statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 20 of 27 countries holding critical-level reserves in at least one fuel category as oil prices surge 44% since February.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces mounting pressure as strategic reserves remain depleted following recent Middle East supply disruptions. While EU-wide averages show petrol at 99.2 days and diesel at 97.6 days—both above the 90-day mandatory minimum—these figures mask severe shortfalls at the national level. A total of 20 of 27 EU countries currently hold critical-level reserves (below 90 days) in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg showing critical status across all three fuel types.

Jet fuel stocks present the most acute vulnerability, averaging just 91.4 days EU-wide and falling to critical levels in 17 countries. Romania faces the most severe situation with only 33.2 days of petrol, 32.4 days of diesel, and 76.4 days of jet fuel remaining. Even major economies like Germany (52.7 days petrol, 64.2 days diesel) and France (64.7 days petrol) are operating well below minimum thresholds in key categories.

The deteriorating reserve situation coincides with a sharp 44% surge in Brent crude prices since late February, climbing from $72.48 to $104.38 per barrel. This has pushed average petrol prices to €1.764 per liter and diesel to €1.943 per liter across the EU, with Netherlands consumers facing the highest petrol costs at €2.279 per liter. The combination of depleted strategic reserves and elevated prices leaves the EU vulnerable to further supply disruptions.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries hold critical-level reserves in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg critical across all three fuel types
Jet fuel shows the greatest vulnerability with 17 countries below 90-day thresholds and an EU average of just 91.4 days
Major economies including Germany and France hold critically low petrol reserves at 52.7 and 64.7 days respectively
Brent crude has surged 44% since late February to $104.38/barrel, driving average EU petrol prices to €1.764 per liter
Only 7 countries maintain safe reserves across all fuel categories, concentrated primarily in Nordic and Baltic regions
